content.problemStatement:
Let's define a function P(A)P(A)P(A) for a sequence AAA as the number of ways to divide AAA into contiguous subsequences such that each element of AAA belongs to exactly one of these subsequences and the sum of elements of each subsequence is between LLL and RRR inclusive.

For each integer nnn between 111 and NNN inclusive, solve the following problem: Consider a random sequence AAA with length nnn where each element is an integer between 111 and KKK chosen uniformly randomly and independently. Find the expected value of P(A)P(A)P(A).

For each valid nnn, it can be proved that the expected value can be expressed as a fraction P/QP / QP/Q, where PPP and QQQ are positive integers and QQQ is coprime with 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353. You should compute P‚čÖQ‚ąí1P \cdot Q^{-1}P‚čÖQ‚ąí1 modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353, where Q‚ąí1Q^{-1}Q‚ąí1 is the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚čÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚čÖ105
the sum of KKK over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains four space-separated integers NNN, KKK, LLL and RRR.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should be P‚čÖQ‚ąí1P \cdot Q^{-1}P‚čÖQ‚ąí1 modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353 for n=in = in=i.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: For n=1n = 1n=1, we have P([1])=P([2])=1P([1]) = P([2]) = 1P([1])=P([2])=1 and P(A)=0P(A) = 0P(A)=0 for any other sequence. Hence, the expected value is 2/2=12 / 2 = 12/2=1.
For n=2n = 2n=2, there are four valid sequences [1,1][1,1][1,1], [1,2][1,2][1,2], [2,1][2,1][2,1] and [2,2][2,2][2,2]. Each of them can be split into one-element subsequences. In addition, [1,1][1,1][1,1] can be left unchanged (split into only one subsequence), so P([1,1])=2P([1,1]) = 2P([1,1])=2. The expected value is (2+1+1+1)/4=5/4=748,683,266(2+1+1+1) / 4 = 5/4 = 748,683,266(2+1+1+1)/4=5/4=748,683,266.
